name: Floral Sprigs
description: small independent flower units used as scattered or joined motifs

name: Roses
description: rounded floral motifs with spiral centers, layered petals, and corded outlines

name: Leaves And Vines
description: botanical stems, paired leaves, curling vines, tendrils, and organic branching

name: Scrollwork
description: curling ornamental lines that behave like thread filigree

name: Medallions
description: centralized circular or oval motifs used as focal units

name: Diamonds And Lattices
description: geometric openwork based on diamond cells, grids, and repeated lattice logic

name: Fans And Shells
description: radiating stitch groups forming fan or shell shapes

name: Pineapples
description: teardrop crochet lace motifs made from expanding and contracting chain spaces

name: Spiderwebs
description: radial thread structures with open cells and concentric connections

name: Stars And Rosettes
description: radial lace motifs with points, rings, and symmetry

name: Scalloped Edges
description: repeated rounded border lobes defining lace edges

name: Picots
description: tiny loops or knots that decorate borders, bars, rings, and chains

name: Mesh Grounds
description: regular or irregular open net structures supporting motifs

name: Bars And Brides
description: thread bridges connecting motifs in open lace

name: Corded Outlines
description: heavier threads used to outline motifs and increase relief

name: Fillings And Modes
description: decorative stitches used inside motif zones

name: Edgings And Borders
description: lace systems designed for outer edges

name: Insertions And Bands
description: linear lace strips meant to be inserted into fabric

name: Appliques
description: motif pieces applied to another fabric

name: Allover Repeats
description: motif systems that tile continuously across yardage

name: Radial Doily Logic
description: round-based expansion grammar for doilies and medallions

name: Corner Turns
description: how lace borders negotiate corners without breaking the pattern
